title: Wireless communication impairments correction
abstract: Systems and methods for providing wireless communication impairment correction using non-linear iterative precoding by a transmitter device are disclosed. The transmitter may exploit the non-linear transmit indications, and perform digital non-linear multiple input multiple output (MIMO) precoding of a transmit signal to improve the error vector magnitude (EVM) at the intended receiver device and/or reduce the adjacent channel leakage ratio (ACLR) at the unintended receiver devices. The non- linear transmit indications may comprise amplitude modulation to amplitude modulation (AM-AM) and amplitude modulation to phase modulation (AM-PM) indications. In operation, the non-linear transmit indications may be received from the intended receiver devices or may be measured by the transmitter device.
claims:
Claims [0084] WHAT IS CLAIMED IS:  CLAIMS 1. A method of wireless communication impairment correction, comprising: obtaining, by a transmitter device, amplitude modulation to amplitude modulation (AM- AM) and amplitude modulation to phase modulation (AM-PM) indications for an intended receiver device; and performing, by the transmitter device, non- linear precoding of a transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the obtaining AM- AM and AM-PM indications comprises: receiving, by the transmitter device, at least one of the AM- AM or AM-PM indications from the intended receiver device. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the obtaining AM- AM and AM-PM indications comprises: determining, by the transmitter device, at least one of the AM- AM or AM-PM indications for the intended receiver device. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the performing non- linear precoding of the transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications comprises: precoding the transmit signal by a beamforming precoding circuit of the transmitter device to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device, wherein the beamforming precoding circuit further provides beamforming for linear time invariant impairments in a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the intended receiver device. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the precoding the transmit signal is performed by the beamforming precoding circuit without digital predistortion being applied to the transmit signal by the transmitter device. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein the transmit path of the transmitter device comprises a plurality of transmit paths for a multiple input multiple output (MEVIO) transmitter device antenna system, and wherein the precoding the transmit signal corrects the transmit signal for antenna array transmission RF impairments for the overall antenna system. 7. The method of claim 4, further comprising: obtaining, by the transmitter device, adjacent channel power leakage indications for one or more unintended receiver devices, wherein the precoding the transmit signal includes beamforming to provide a null with respect to at least one unintended receiver device of the one or more unintended receiver devices. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the obtaining adjacent channel power leakage indications comprises: receiving, by the transmitter device, adjacent channel power leakage indications from one or more unintended receiver devices. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising: generating an error signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications, wherein the performing the non- linear precoding of the transmit signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications is based on the error signal. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: iteratively performing the generating the error signal and the performing non- linear precoding of the transmit signal to provide an adjacent channel leakage ratio (ACLR) below a threshold level at one or more undesired receiver devices and to provide error vector magnitude (EVM) improvement at the desired receiver device. 11. The method of claim 9, further comprising: modeling non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device based at least upon the AM- AM and AM-PM indications, wherein the generating the error signal is based at least in part on a result of the modeling non-linearities in the transmit path of the transmitter device. 12. The method of claim 11 , further comprising: modeling a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the receiver device, wherein the generating the error signal is further based at least in part on a result of the modeling the propagation channel. 13. An apparatus configured for wireless communication impairment correction, comprising: means for obtaining, by a transmitter device, amplitude modulation to amplitude modulation (AM- AM) and amplitude modulation to phase modulation (AM-PM) indications for an intended receiver device; and means for performing, by the transmitter device, non-linear precoding of a transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device. 14. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the means for obtaining AM- AM and AM- PM indications comprises: means for receiving, by the transmitter device, at least one of the AM- AM or AM-PM indications from the intended receiver device. 15. The apparatus of claim 13, wherein the means for performing non-linear precoding of the transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM or AM-PM indications comprises: means for precoding the transmit signal by the transmitter device to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device, wherein the transmitter device further provides beamforming for linear time invariant impairments in a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the intended receiver device. 16. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the means for precoding the transmit signal is performed by the transmitter device without digital predistortion being applied to the transmit signal. 17. The apparatus of claim 15, wherein the transmit path of the transmitter device comprises a plurality of transmit paths for a multiple input multiple output (MEVIO) transmitter device antenna system, and wherein the means for precoding the transmit signal 

 corrects the transmit signal for antenna array transmission RF impairments for the overall antenna system. 18. The apparatus of claim 15, further comprising: means for obtaining, by the transmitter device, adjacent channel power leakage indications for one or more unintended receiver devices, wherein the means for precoding the transmit signal includes beamforming to provide a null with respect to at least one unintended receiver device of the one or more unintended receiver devices. 19. The apparatus of claim 13, further comprising: means for generating an error signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM- PM indications, wherein the means for performing the non- linear precoding of the transmit signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications is based on the error signal. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, further comprising: means for modeling non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device based at least in part upon the AM- AM and AM-PM indications, wherein the generating the error signal is based at least in part on a result of the modeling non-linearities in the transmit path of the transmitter device. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, further comprising: means for modeling a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the receiver device, wherein the means for generating the error signal is further based at least in part on a result of modeling the propagation channel. 22. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having program code recorded thereon, the program code comprising: program code for causing a computer to obtain, by a transmitter device, amplitude modulation to amplitude modulation (AM- AM) and amplitude modulation to phase modulation (AM-PM) indications for an intended receiver device; and perform, by the transmitter device, non- linear precoding of a transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device. 23. The non-transitory computer-readable medium, wherein the program code causing the computer to obtain AM- AM or AM-PM indications comprises program code for causing the computer to receive, by the transmitter device, at least one of the AM- AM or AM-PM indications from an intended receiver device. 24. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 23, wherein the program code for causing the computer to perform non-linear precoding of the transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications comprises program code for causing the computer to: precode the transmit signal to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device and for linear time invariant impairments in a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the intended receiver device. 25. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 23, wherein the program code further causes the computer to: generate an error signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications, wherein the non-linear precoding of the transmit signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications is based on the error signal. 26. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 25, wherein the program code further cases the computer to: iteratively generate the error signal and perform the non-linear precoding of the transmit signal to provide an adjacent channel leakage ratio (ACLR) below a threshold level at one or more undesired receiver devices and to provide error vector magnitude (EVM) improvement at the desired receiver device. 27. An apparatus configured for wireless communication impairment correction, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and a memory coupled to the at least one processor, wherein the at least one processor is configured: to obtain, at a transmitter device, amplitude modulation to amplitude modulation (AM- AM) and amplitude modulation to phase modulation (AM-PM) indications for an intended receiver device; and to perform non- linear precoding of a transmit signal for the intended receiver 

 device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the at least one processor configured to obtain AM- AM and AM-PM indications is configured: to receive, at a transmitter device, at least one of the AM- AM or AM-PM indications from an intended receiver device. 29. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the at least one processor configured to perform non- linear precoding of the transmit signal for the intended receiver device based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications is configured: to precode the transmit signal to correct the transmit signal for non-linearities in a transmit path of the transmitter device and for linear time invariant impairments in a propagation channel between the transmitter device and the intended receiver device. 30. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the at least one processor is configured: to generate an error signal based at least in part on the AM- AM and AM-PM indications, wherein non-linear precoding of the transmit signal based at least in part on AM- AM and AM-PM indications is based on the error signal.
